content.thinking:
The user is investigating whether GoldTarget (which only appears in FreeGame) goes through NearWin logic. A grep was performed to search for patterns related to `m_isHasScatterNearWin` and `IsFreeGame` in the SlotReels.ts file.

Key findings from the grep results:
1. Lines 1079-1080, 1888-1892, and 3784-3785 all show the same pattern: `m_isHasScatterNearWin` is set to true only when `!this.m_gameView.IsFreeGame` (i.e., NOT in FreeGame)
2. This means the NearWin logic is explicitly disabled during FreeGame mode
3. The condition appears multiple times: scatter count is in the near-win range AND not in FreeGame

This is a discovery about how the game logic works - specifically that NearWin functionality is disabled during FreeGame, which directly answers the user's question about whether GoldTarget (which only appears in FreeGame) would go through NearWin.

Type: discovery (learning about existing system)
Title: NearWin logic disabled during FreeGame mode
Subtitle: Scatter NearWin feature only activates in MainGame, never in FreeGame
Facts:
- m_isHasScatterNearWin set to true only when !this.m_gameView.IsFreeGame
- NearWin condition requires scatter count between SCATTER_NEARWIN_COLLECT_COUNT and SCATTER_NEARWIN_MAX_COUNT
- Multiple locations in SlotReels.ts enforce the !IsFreeGame check (lines 1079, 1888, 3784)
Narrative: Investigation revealed that the NearWin logic for scatter symbols is explicitly disabled during FreeGame mode. Multiple code locations (lines 1079-1080, 1888-1892, 3784-3785) show that m_isHasScatterNearWin is only set to true when the game is NOT in FreeGame mode (!this.m_gameView.IsFreeGame). This means GoldTarget symbols, which only appear during FreeGame, will never trigger or go through the NearWin logic path. 

This is a MainGame-only feature, so the NearWin mechanics don't apply to FreeGame scenarios at all.

content.thinking:
This read shows code handling Target and GoldTarget symbol falling animations. The user is reading a section that deals with creating spine animations for Target symbols when they land. This includes:

1. Detection of Target or GoldTarget symbols
2. Finding existing target spine nodes in the same column
3. Either tweening existing spines to new positions or creating new ones after fall completes
4. Registration of target stop nodes with the EffectPlate

This is discovering how Target/GoldTarget symbols are visually handled during the falling/landing animation phase. It's part of understanding the game's visual effect system for these special symbols.

This is a discovery about how the Target symbol animation system works - reusing existing spine nodes when available and creating new ones when needed.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Target symbol spine animation reuse during falls</title>
  <subtitle>Target and GoldTarget falling animation reuses existing spine nodes in same column</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>SlotReels.ts handles both Symbol.Target and Symbol.GoldTarget during fall animation at line 1913</fact>
    <fact>System searches for existing Target spine nodes in same column using 30-pixel X-position tolerance</fact>
    <fact>Existing spine nodes are tweened to new positions with column and row delay calculations</fact>
    <fact>New spine nodes created via EffectPlate.ReelStopEffect only when no existing spine found in column</fact>
    <fact>EffectPlate.RegisterTargetStopNode called after tween completion to track final position</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The Target symbol animation system implements an optimization that reuses existing spine animations within the same column. When a Target or GoldTarget symbol lands (lines 1913-1962), the code searches the EffectPlate's registered target nodes for any spine already in that column (within 30 pixels on X-axis). If found, the existing spine is tweened to follow the symbol to its new position with appropriate fall delays based on column and row. If no existing spine is found in that column, a new one is created after the fall animation completes. This approach prevents creating duplicate spine animations and ensures smooth visual continuity when Target symbols cascade down during drop sequences.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
  </concepts>

    <file>assets/Script/SlotReels.ts</file>
  </files_read>

  </files_modified>

<observation>
  <type>change</type>
  <title>Added diagnostic logging for Target symbol spine reuse during falls</title>
  <subtitle>Track when existing Target spine animations are reused without playing IN animation</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Log statement added at line 1937 in SlotReels.ts StartFallAllVisibleSymbolsByServerPlateInfo method</fact>
    <fact>Log tracks the "else branch" where existing Target spine nodes are reused during cascade falls</fact>
    <fact>Log message includes column, row, symbolId, symbol type (Gold/Normal), and existingKey identifier</fact>
    <fact>Diagnostic tag [TargetDiag] used to filter Target-related animation flow logs</fact>
    <fact>Message indicates this path does not play IN animation because spine follows existing node</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Diagnostic logging was added to track Target symbol animation behavior during cascade falls. When a Target or GoldTarget symbol lands during the fall sequence, the system searches for existing spine animations in the same column to reuse. This log statement marks the branch where an existing spine is found and tweened to follow the symbol to its new position. The Chinese message "一般分支『未播IN(沿用既有spine跟隨掉落)』" indicates this is the "normal branch" where no IN animation is played because the existing spine animation is reused. This logging appears to be part of investigating whether GoldTarget symbols (which only appear in FreeGame) properly handle animation state transitions, particularly in relation to the NearWin logic that is disabled during FreeGame mode.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>assets/Script/SlotReels.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

  <type>change</type>
  <title>Added diagnostic logging for new Target spine creation during falls</title>
  <subtitle>Track when new Target spine animations are created via ReelStopEffect with IN animation</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Log statement added at line 1953 in SlotReels.ts StartFallAllVisibleSymbolsByServerPlateInfo method</fact>
    <fact>Log tracks the else branch where no existing Target spine is found in the column</fact>
    <fact>Message indicates new spine will be created after symbol lands via ReelStopEffect which plays IN animation</fact>

    <fact>Diagnostic tag [TargetDiag] used consistently across Target animation flow logging</fact>
